title: Session restore with valid CLIENT token routes app to client home

preconditions.description: Expo web app is served at http://localhost:8081 via npm run web.

Before the app loads, localStorage key 'auth_token' is seeded with a mock JWT string via Playwright addInitScript. Confirmed key: store/session.ts uses TOKEN_KEY = 'auth_token'; expo-secure-store on web stores with the raw key in localStorage (no prefix).
Playwright intercepts GET /users/me and returns HTTP 200 with a CLIENT user body before the page navigates.

testData.name: mockClientToken
testData.notes: Placeholder JWT string. Real value not required — only used as a Bearer token header in the intercepted /users/me call.

journey.notes: App launches at session restore splash (app/index.tsx). LoadingScreen component is shown while readPersistedToken() and GET /users/me execute.
Wait for the mocked GET /users/me request to complete. This ensures session restore has received the user payload.
After getMe() resolves with CLIENT user, app calls router.replace('/(client)/home'). Expo Router resolves this to a route containing /home.

assertions.notes: Confirm session restore called GET /users/me with the seeded token. Full URL is http://localhost:3000/users/me per EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL default in lib/config.ts.
Route must contain 'home', confirming /(client)/home was reached.
The client home stub renders 'Client Home — Milestone 2'. Partial text 'Client Home' is sufficient.
Login stub text must not be visible — the app must not have fallen through to the auth group.

notes: expo-secure-store on web stores items in localStorage with the raw key — no prefix. Confirmed key: 'auth_token' (TOKEN_KEY constant in store/session.ts).
The Playwright addInitScript approach must seed the token before the app JS runs so readPersistedToken() finds the value on first load.
The client layout guard (app/(client)/_layout.tsx) checks user.role === 'CLIENT'. If the mocked /users/me returns role=CLIENT, the guard passes and home.tsx renders.
home.tsx in M01 is a stub. The observable text 'Client Home — Milestone 2' is the expected visible content.
